mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 90% of its 2017 baseline signals a strengthening supply chain that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 6% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 900,000 units signals a strengthening demand for residential real estate that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 130 million signals a strengthening labor market that compresses wage growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 255 signals a strengthening inflationary pressure that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 190 billion signals a strengthening supply chain that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict positive returns in 60% of instances.
